summary: The aim is that students develop general communicative competence and learn to apply this productively and receptively in authentic spoken interactions. English Conversation is a practical course in which students practice skills to express themselves in a foreign language and negotiate meaning in communicative conversational settings. Students will use the English they have previously learned.
aims:
English Conversation I is a practice-based course aimed at developing conversational skills in English. Course objectives are to:
•develop English communication ability, emphasizing speaking and listening skills
•develop an operational understanding of grammar, particularly spoken grammar
•improve English pronunciation skills (both segmental and suprasegmental/prosody)
•activate previously learned English and improve fluency
•build awareness of the socially appropriate use of language according to context
•develop ability to negotiate meaning in communication
•develop cultural understanding as it relates to English language use
•foster intercultural understanding
•provide students with an English-only intercultural classroom experience
goals: Develop English communication ability, emphasizing speaking and listening skills
Develop an operational understanding of grammar, particularly spoken grammar
Activate previously learned English and improve fluency
Learn about socially appropriate use of language according to context (develop basic pragmatic ability in English)
Develop ability to negotiate meaning in communication

plan.text: Orientation: Course outline, expectations and goals, introduction of speaking assessment rubric, disctribution of Eikaiwa Handbook
Getting to Know You (GTKY) activities and introduction to small group conversation
Getting to Know You (GTKY) Hometown and Familiy
Getting to Know You (GTKY) Diet and health
Getting to Know You (GTKY) Sports, music, movies and entertainment
Travel Unit Task / Choosing a foreign country task
Travel Unit Task / Choosing a study abroad program task
Mid-term assessment and feedback using KU rubric, first half of class
Mid-term assessment and feedback using KU rubric, second half of class
Focus on language function 1: Asking and giving advice
Focus on language function 2: Clarifying and negotiating meaning
Focus on language function 3: Recounting experiences and telling stories
Focus on language function 4: Explaining and describing
Focus on language function 5: Expressing preferences, attitudes and feelings
Final course assessment 1: (using KU speaking assessment rubric)
Final course assessment 2: (using KU speaking assessment rubric)

prereq: This course is available to all first-year students and is a requirement for graduation.
prep: Students are required to review and practice all classroom learning and do preparatory homework assignments. They will also be asked to do submit audio recordings of their conversations for selected classroom tasks.
